steps.title: Examine the shape of each histogram
steps.content:
Company A: All data is clustered between 25–40 thousand units, with the peak at 30–35. The three bars have frequencies of approximately 43, 48, and 41. This is a narrow, concentrated distribution.

Company B: Data spreads across the entire range from 15–50 thousand units, with frequencies of approximately 31, 22, 12, 5, 12, 22, and 31. This creates a bimodal shape with peaks at the extremes (15–20 and 45–50) and a valley in the middle (30–35).

steps.title: Apply the standard deviation principle
steps.content:
Standard deviation measures spread—the typical distance of data points from the mean.
  • Narrow, concentrated distribution → values cluster close to the mean → small standard deviation
  • Wide, spread-out distribution → values scatter far from the mean → large standard deviation
Company A is tightly concentrated in a narrow range; Company B is widely spread from 15 to 50. Therefore, Company A's standard deviation is less than Company B's standard deviation. That matches choice A — A is correct.
